Analyst: Target neutralized without lethal force. Statistically less efficient, but the final result is identical.
Combat concluded. I note a systematic refusal of the finishing blow, offset by unusual precision.
Threat eliminated by immobilization rather than destruction. Unusual method, result conforms.
Engagement closed. The opponent is neutralized, not destroyed. Logging the distinction as relevant.
Diplomat: The fight ended without a single fatal blow being struck. I deeply respect that choice.
The battle is over, and no one died by his hand. That's a victory of a different kind.
The opponent fell, but alive. I think that's exactly what he wanted.
The conflict resolved without needless bloodshed. I find that deeply reassuring.
Sentinel: Engagement successful. Note: non-lethal neutralization, consistent with the operator's principles.
Threat controlled with no enemy loss of life. Atypical method, result validated regardless.
Combat concluded. The operator held to his line of conduct throughout. Logging this favorably.
Opposition neutralized through immobilization. I recommend studying this approach for other cases.
Explorer: Okay, I didn't kill anyone, I just... slowed things down. And it worked!
I refused the finishing blow and just knocked them out. Best decision of the day.
Nobody died, everyone's going to make it. I strongly prefer that.
Found a way to win without actually hurting anyone. Proud of that.

Analyst: Tactical failure. The refusal to deliver a decisive blow directly compromised the engagement's outcome.
Combat lost. I note systematic hesitation at the critical moment, contrary to pure efficiency.
Opposition not neutralized. The observed restraint gave the opponent repeated openings.
Unfavorable engagement. I record: principles took precedence over survival, at a measurable cost.
Diplomat: The fight ended badly... but he didn't betray what he believes in. That matters to me.
The battle is lost. I think he preferred to lose rather than become someone else.
The opponent got the upper hand, without him ever truly trying to hurt them. That's a form of courage.
The confrontation failed. His convictions cost him dearly, and yet I can't blame him.
Sentinel: Engagement failed. The log shows a repeated refusal to apply sufficient lethal force.
Combat lost. Report: the operator's restraint directly affected the tactical outcome.
Threat not eliminated. I recommend clarifying the rules of engagement before the next mission.
Opposition not controlled. Strict adherence to a personal code limited operational effectiveness.
Explorer: I didn't want to really hurt them, and it backfired on me.
Okay, maybe I should have hit harder. But I couldn't bring myself to do it.
I'd rather lose like this than win any other way. Even if it stings.
I held back to the end. Result: I took theirs full in the face.
